rationale: [wallclock-only] deferred folding in AirSumcheckSession — fuse fold+compute via prev_folding_factor. +15% regression: fold_and_sumcheck_compute path reads 2x more data per round (unfolded arrays are twice the size), and sumcheck_fold_and_compute_core for BasePacked lacks the split_eq optimization, falling back to per-element eq_at closure
fuse alpha into GKR quotient split_eq — reduce 4 accumulators to 2 in inner loop. -0.045% iai, below 0.10% threshold. GKR quotient is ~5% e2e but instruction savings too small.

pass &[IF] slices to eval_fn instead of Vec<IF> in sumcheck inner loop. REGRESSED +0.87% iai: explicit indexing+pre-alloc generates worse code than collect(). Confirms dead end from exp1 iters 3,34: compiler optimizes functional collect() better than imperative buffer reuse.

[wallclock-only] batch MLE eval — precompute eq table via eval_eq once per table point, replace column evaluates with dot products. +13% regression: 2.5MB eq table thrashes L2/L3 cache when scanned 20+ times for dot products vs recursive eval's cache-friendly folding pattern

[wallclock-only] deferred folding in AirSumcheckSession — fuse fold+compute via prev_folding_factor with split_eq for BasePacked. +22% regression: per-element base→extension promotion (EFP*PFP) in inner loop d+1 times per element costs more than the saved memory pass. Eager fold-then-compute is better at scale.

fuse alpha into GKR quotient inner loop — absorb alpha*u2+u0 before eq_val multiply. iai -1.92% (real instruction savings) but +9% wall-clock: alpha_bc*u2 adds serial dependency to critical path, destroying ILP between independent single/double terms. OoO CPU prefers independent parallel paths over shorter serial chains.

[wallclock-only] batch column evals in logup post-GKR — precompute eq(inner_point, ·) table once, dot-product all base-field columns. +9.4% regression: 1.25MB eq table (EF=quintic, 5×4B×2^16) thrashes L2/L3 when scanned 20+ times per table. Same cache issue as iter 4. Recursive fold cache-friendly access pattern wins.

split_eq optimization for BasePacked sumcheck compute — avoids per-element get_packed(i) (broadcast+EFP×EFP mul). Real -2.07% instruction savings, but iai FAIL: any code change around sumcheck_compute_core closure creates new monomorphization hash (Rust anonymous closure types are position-dependent). Even single match arm with if-else changes closure position. Optimization saves ~130 instructions/element but get_packed is <1% of per-element cost (eval_fn dominates at ~5000 instr/call × degree), so wall-clock impact ~0.2% — below -1.5% [wallclock-only] threshold.

[wallclock-only] split_eq for BasePacked sumcheck — dedicated sumcheck_compute_with_split_eq_base avoids per-element get_packed. +11% regression: separate function prevents compiler from inlining eval_fn into sumcheck_compute_core monomorphization — loses cross-closure optimizations. Same code in separate function = different monomorphization = worse codegen.

[wallclock-only] parallel session computation in batched AIR sumcheck — compute bare polys for 3 sessions via rayon par_iter_mut instead of sequentially. +8.6% regression: rayon par_iter_mut overhead (task spawning, work-stealing synchronization) dominates when sessions already use nested par_iter internally. 3 outer tasks with 8 cores = no utilization gain, only contention and cache pollution from concurrent multilinear scans.
